Title: Chromosome-level assembly and analysis of three hydroxy fatty acid-producing Physaria species

Common_Genes.Source_Species: Physaria lindheimeri; Physaria pallida; Physaria fendleri
Common_Genes.Source_Species_Latin_Name: Physaria lindheimeri; Physaria pallida; Physaria fendleri
Common_Genes.Applied_Species: Physaria lindheimeri; Physaria pallida; Physaria fendleri
Common_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Physaria lindheimeri; Physaria pallida; Physaria fendleri
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: lesquerolic acid (C20:1-OH); hydroxy fatty acids in TAG
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: industrial feedstock for lubricants, motor oils, desiccants, nylon, plastics, cosmetics, pharmaceuticals, and polishes
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: LPAT is implicated in incorporation of hydroxy fatty acids into TAG and is described as a major limit for HFA accumulation in TAG molecules in P. fendleri; expression levels were similar among species and sequence modeling did not support obvious catalytic-pocket differences.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. LPAT genes were highly expressed in developing seeds, especially early to middle stages. 2. LPAT specifically incorporates acyl groups into the sn-2 position of the glycerol backbone. 3. LPAT was discussed as a likely factor limiting HFA accumulation in P. fendleri TAG, but this paper did not directly validate LPAT as the causal gene for species differences.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq expression profiling; sequence comparison; substrate docking model inference
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: high in developing seeds, especially early to middle stages
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: high in developing seeds, especially early to middle stages; low in leaf and stem
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included as a metabolism-relevant common gene because it affects HFA incorporation into storage TAG, but direct causal evidence in this paper is limited and authors state further biochemical characterization is required.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for engineering improved HFA incorporation into TAG, especially sn-2 positioning, but requires biochemical validation before deployment.

Pathway_Genes.Source_Species: Physaria lindheimeri; Physaria pallida; Physaria fendleri
Pathway_Genes.Source_Species_Latin_Name: Physaria lindheimeri; Physaria pallida; Physaria fendleri
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species: Physaria lindheimeri; Physaria pallida; Physaria fendleri; Arabidopsis
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Physaria lindheimeri; Physaria pallida; Physaria fendleri; Arabidopsis thaliana
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: hydroxy fatty acid biosynthesis; seed oil biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: oleic acid hydroxylation on phosphatidylcholine leading to HFA formation
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: upstream; committed formation step for HFA precursor
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: hydroxylates oleic acid at the Δ12 position on phosphatidylcholine to form ricinoleic acid
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms immediate precursor of lesquerolic acid and directly determines HFA synthesis capacity
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: supported as a key control step for HFA production; lower PfFAH12 activity is linked to lower HFA accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: industrial feedstock for lubricants, motor oils, desiccants, nylon, plastics, cosmetics, pharmaceuticals, and polishes
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Higher-activity PlFAH12 and PpFAH12 promote greater ricinoleic acid formation and are associated with much higher lesquerolic acid accumulation than PfFAH12; mutating PfFAH12 residues H111Q and Q225H increases hydroxylation activity.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FAH12 is highly expressed in middle and late developing seeds and negligibly expressed in leaf and stem in all three Physaria species. 2. Despite high transcript abundance, PfFAH12 shows substantially lower hydroxylase activity than PlFAH12 and PpFAH12 in mammalian cells. 3. Two residues in PfFAH12, H111 and Q225, underlie reduced activity; H111Q/Q225H increases ricinoleic acid production by nearly 2.5-fold. 4. Arabidopsis lines expressing PlFAH12 or PpFAH12 accumulate more ricinoleic acid in seeds than lines expressing PfFAH12, confirming enzyme-dependent differences in vivo.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq expression profiling; heterologous expression in mammalian cells; protein purification; GC-FID product assay; site-directed mutagenesis; AlphaFold structural modeling and docking; Arabidopsis seed-specific overexpression; seed fatty acid profiling
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: membrane-bound fatty acid desaturase/hydroxylase family with histidine motifs
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: high in middle and late developing seeds; negligible in early seed, leaf and stem relative to later seeds
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: high in developing seeds; low/negligible in leaf and stem; transgene active in Arabidopsis seeds under napin promoter
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: seed-specific napin promoter in Arabidopsis overexpression lines
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Authors note PfFAH12 may be bifunctional with both hydroxylation and desaturation activities based on prior reports; this paper primarily validates hydroxylation differences. Key residues affecting activity are H111 and Q225 in PfFAH12 versus Q111 and H225 in PlFAH12/PpFAH12.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value engineering target for increasing HFA production. Prefer PlFAH12/PpFAH12 or edited PfFAH12 alleles with improved catalytic residues; can be stacked with TAG assembly genes for higher commercial HFA accumulation.

Pathway_Genes.Source_Species: Physaria lindheimeri; Physaria pallida; Physaria fendleri
Pathway_Genes.Source_Species_Latin_Name: Physaria lindheimeri; Physaria pallida; Physaria fendleri
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species: Physaria lindheimeri; Physaria pallida; Physaria fendleri
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Physaria lindheimeri; Physaria pallida; Physaria fendleri
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: very-long-chain hydroxy fatty acid elongation; fatty acid elongase complex
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: elongation of C18 hydroxy fatty acid to C20 hydroxy fatty acid
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: downstream of FAH12; elongation step toward terminal product
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: ricinoleic acid-derived C18 hydroxy acyl-CoA / C18-HFA substrate
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: catalyzes condensation/elongation of C18 hydroxy fatty acid substrate to generate C20 hydroxy fatty acid within the fatty acid elongase complex
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly forms target product from immediate precursor
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: not established as limiting in this study; authors speculate KCS3 is likely not limiting because ricinoleic acid is almost completely converted to lesquerolic acid
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: industrial feedstock for lubricants, motor oils, desiccants, nylon, plastics, cosmetics, pharmaceuticals, and polishes
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: KCS3 is strongly expressed in middle and late developing seeds and supports efficient conversion of ricinoleic acid to lesquerolic acid, but was not shown here to explain interspecies differences.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. KCS3 is one of the two key enzymes directly involved in lesquerolic acid biosynthesis. 2. KCS3 transcripts are highly enriched in middle and late developing seeds, with negligible expression in vegetative tissues. 3. The near-complete conversion of ricinoleic acid to lesquerolic acid suggests KCS3 is unlikely the metabolic bottleneck among the three species in this study.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq expression profiling; fatty acid composition analysis; pathway inference from prior biochemical knowledge
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: 3-ketoacyl-CoA synthase; condensing enzyme of fatty acid elongase complex
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: high in middle and late developing seeds; negligible in leaf and stem
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: high in middle and late developing seeds; negligible in leaf and stem
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Physaria KCS3 is described as highly homologous to AtKCS18/AtFAE1 but specifically catalyzes elongation of C18-HFA. Included as a core pathway gene because it is highlighted in Results as a key HFA synthesis gene, although mechanistic dissection in this paper focused more heavily on FAH12.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful stacking target with FAH12 and TAG assembly genes to engineer higher lesquerolic acid production in oilseeds.

Regulation_Genes.Source_Species: Physaria lindheimeri; Physaria pallida; Physaria fendleri
Regulation_Genes.Source_Species_Latin_Name: Physaria lindheimeri; Physaria pallida; Physaria fendleri
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Species: Physaria lindheimeri; Physaria pallida; Physaria fendleri
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Physaria lindheimeri; Physaria pallida; Physaria fendleri
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: seed-enriched central regulator associated with lipid biosynthesis and seed oil accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: hydroxy fatty acids; lesquerolic acid-containing seed oil
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Seed-high expression of WRI1 is associated with activation of lipid biosynthesis supporting HFA-rich oil accumulation, but no direct perturbation was performed in this paper.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. WRI1 was identified among five central lipid-biosynthesis TFs with much higher expression in developing seeds than in leaf and stem. 2. Its expression pattern supports a regulatory role in seed oil/HFA biosynthesis background in Physaria. 3. Functional causality was inferred from known lipid regulatory role rather than directly tested here.
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: high in developing seeds; low in leaf and stem
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: high in developing seeds; low in leaf and stem
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Mentioned as a central lipid regulator in Results, but not directly functionally dissected in this study.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential regulatory engineering target to enhance fatty acid synthesis flux in conjunction with FAH12 and downstream acyltransferases.

Regulation_Genes.Source_Species: Physaria lindheimeri; Physaria pallida; Physaria fendleri

Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: central regulator associated with seed oil accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: hydroxy fatty acids; lesquerolic acid-containing seed oil
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Seed-high expression is associated with lipid biosynthesis in developing seeds.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ABI3 showed much higher expression in developing seeds than in leaf and stem. 2. The expression pattern is consistent with a central role in regulating seed oil biosynthesis in Physaria.
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: high in developing seeds; low in leaf and stem
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: high in developing seeds; low in leaf and stem
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: No direct functional validation in this paper.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential seed-oil regulatory target for combinatorial engineering.

Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: high in developing seeds; low in leaf and stem
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: No direct functional validation in this paper.
